summary: Frontend-only verification of the dark-mode trust strip band CSS fix on the homepage. Verified all three test scenarios from the review request: (1) light mode renders white bg with dark text, (2) dark mode (via theme toggle) renders the dark navy linear-gradient bg with light text/icons, and (3) the moon/sun navbar theme-toggle correctly switches the trust strip styling in real time without page refresh. The CSS specificity fix scoping `html:not([data-bs-theme="dark"]) body:not(.adm) .hero.hero--zoom-navy + .py-3 { background:#fff !important; }` to light mode only is working as intended.
critical_code_review_comments: Server-side cookie detection for `uc_theme` cookie set before the first navigation request did NOT result in data-bs-theme=dark in our test (the html still rendered with data-bs-theme=light). This may be a Playwright/preview-gateway cookie-forwarding quirk rather than an app bug, since once the cookie is set and the page is reloaded normally (or theme toggle is clicked), the dark theme renders correctly. Worth a follow-up check in /app/php-version/includes/header.php to confirm the cookie name + path used server-side exactly matches the JS toggle and to ensure SameSite settings allow cross-site preview reloads.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Trust strip band fix confirmed via Playwright. Key evidence from the toggle test: before click data-bs-theme=light and trust-strip-band bg-color=rgb(255,255,255); after click data-bs-theme=dark and trust-strip-band bg-image=linear-gradient(rgb(5,11,27) 0%, rgb(11,20,48) 100%). Theme toggle selector that worked: [data-testid="theme-toggle"] (class: btn btn-sm btn-outline-secondary rounded-circle). The trust-strip-band element has data-testid="trust-strip-band" and contains the three labels SSL Secured, 30-Day Guarantee, Microsoft Verified.
rca of the issue: The original bug was caused by the CSS rule `body:not(.adm) .hero.hero--zoom-navy + .py-3 { background:#fff !important; }` having higher specificity (0,4,1) than the dark-mode override `[data-bs-theme="dark"] body:not(.adm) .trust-strip-band` (0,3,1). The fix scopes the white-bg rule to `html:not([data-bs-theme="dark"])` so it now ONLY applies in light mode, allowing the dark-mode rule to take effect. Verified visually: dark-mode trust band now shows the expected navy gradient (rgb(5,11,27) -> rgb(11,20,48)) and text is readable in white.
